objective: Adopt the closed-world project/module workflow without changing Spatial Camera Panel package, permission, source, or runtime behavior.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route Spatial Camera Panel work through its app-local composition, feature lock, and current unit.
Expose the app-local workflow, disabled-by-default feature families, and candidate records.
Record the first adopted downstream project workspace.
instruction_surfaces.validation: portable and installed skill phrase checks

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route app-specific architecture work through the composition and feature lock before extraction.
instruction_surfaces.validation: portable and installed skill phrase checks

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Include the app-local workflow surface in future graph audits.
instruction_surfaces.validation: portable and installed skill phrase checks

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Record the behavior-neutral downstream adoption invariant.
Expose the first public downstream adoption proof.

non_scope: APK, manifest, permission, profile, property, or intent changes.
Promotion of particle, hand, camera, or media candidates.

acceptance.proof: The base panel shell is the only enabled workflow feature; six nearby families are explicit disabled entries.
Remote peer media is absent from both project modules and the feature lock, and the static gate rejects its accidental appearance.
The existing Spatial static gate and native surface-particle host tests still pass without device work.
Rusty Quest routers plus installed and portable workflow skills name the adopted workspace.
acceptance.command: instruction phrase checks and git diff --check

outputs: App-local composition, feature lock, candidates, compact state, event projection, and receipt.
Self-contained workflow static gate integrated into the app validation wrapper.
Synchronized repo routers and relevant installed/portable skills.
commit_policy: Commit the validated workflow adoption and instruction synchronization as one Rusty Quest slice.
